the probability that a randomly selected student from group b has a higher engagement level than a randomly selected student from group a is calculated by finding the probability that x_b > x_a. this is done by finding the probability that x_b - x_a > 0. since x_a and x_b are independent normal variables, the difference x_b - x_a is also normally distributed with mean μ_b - μ_a = 75 - 70 = 5 and variance σ_b² + σ_a² = 12² + 10² = 144 + 100 = 244. the standard deviation of the difference is sqrt(244) ≈ 15.62. the probability that x_b - x_a > 0 is the probability that a standard normal variable is greater than -5/15.62 ≈ -0.32. this probability is approximately 0.6255. the probability that a randomly selected student from group b has a higher engagement level than a randomly selected student from group a is 0.6255.
